messages.content: Build a basic Stream pipeline from scratch using Stream.make, Stream.map, and Stream.filter to keep only doubled even numbers.
Build from scratch an Effect v3 Stream that starts with five string tags, uppercases each, and filters out any shorter than three characters.
What's the Effect v3 way to compose Stream.map and Stream.filter on a Stream.make source? Show a minimal typed example that squares odd numbers only.
Write an Effect v3 Stream that reads a typed User[] (Stream.make), keeps adults, and projects their name. Constraints: strict types, no any, use pipe and Stream.runCollect.

Explain briefly how Stream.map and Stream.filter compose in Effect v3, then show a typed implementation that turns a numeric Stream into a comma-separated string of positives.

Stream.map and Stream.filter both return a new Stream rather than mutating the source, so you can chain them inside pipe() like any data-first combinator. Filter drops elements that fail the predicate before map sees them, which keeps the inner transform total even when the source has dirty data.

Refactor this imperative array pipeline to an Effect v3 Stream using Stream.make, Stream.map, and Stream.filter:

const xs = [1,2,3,4,5]; const out = xs.filter(n => n > 2).map(n => n * 10);
Build an Effect v3 Stream.mapEffect pipeline from scratch that fetches a simulated score for each user id and keeps the ordered results.
What's the Effect v3 way to run an Effect per stream element? Show a minimal example using Stream.mapEffect with a tagged error.
Build from scratch a Stream.mapEffect pipeline that parses each string to a number, failing the stream with a tagged error on the first non-numeric input.
Explain briefly how Stream.mapEffect differs from Stream.map in Effect v3, then implement a small example that logs each element before emitting it downstream.

Add concurrency 4 to this Stream.mapEffect pipeline so requests fan out in parallel:

const out = pipe(ids, Stream.mapEffect(fetchUser), Stream.runCollect);
Write an Effect v3 Stream pipeline that validates each inbound record via Stream.mapEffect. Constraints: tagged error class, no any, preserve typed error channel to the consumer.
Build from scratch an Effect v3 program that broadcasts one source Stream to two consumers using Stream.broadcast, one computing the sum and one the count.
What's the Effect v3 way to share one upstream Stream with multiple consumers without double-pulling? Show a minimal example using Stream.share.

Explain briefly how Stream.broadcast avoids re-pulling the source in Effect v3, then show a typed implementation that routes a number stream to a min fiber and a max fiber.

Broadcast subscribes multiple downstream consumers to an internal hub so each element is pulled from the source exactly once and fanned out.

Add a second consumer to this shared stream that writes elements to a log via Effect.logInfo. Current code:

const shared = yield* Stream.share(src, { capacity: 8 });
Write an Effect v3 program that broadcasts a source stream to three consumers (sum, count, max). Constraints: strict types, no any, use Effect.all with concurrency so consumers run in parallel.
Build from scratch an Effect v3 stream that throttles incoming events to at most 3 per second using Stream.throttle.
What's the Effect v3 way to convert a Stream of elements into a Stream of fixed-size chunks? Show a minimal example using Stream.grouped.
Build from scratch an Effect v3 Stream pipeline that reads chunks of a source via Stream.chunks and sums each chunk independently.

Explain briefly how Stream.throttle controls pull rate in Effect v3, then show a typed example that throttles a stream of payloads to 100 units per second where cost equals payload length.

Throttle uses a token-bucket: each pulled element consumes `cost(a)` tokens from a bucket that refills `units` tokens per `duration`, suspending the producer when the bucket is empty.
Write an Effect v3 Stream that groups integers into chunks of 4 then emits only chunks whose sum exceeds 10. Constraints: strict types, no any, pipe composition.

Add throttling of 5 per second to this existing stream so downstream can't be overwhelmed:

const out = pipe(src, Stream.runCollect);
Build from scratch a Stream → Sink pipeline that counts the elements emitted by a Stream.make source using Sink.count.
What's the Effect v3 way to sum a stream of numbers into a single value? Show a minimal typed example using Sink.sum.
Build from scratch an Effect v3 program that drives a Stream through Sink.collectAll and projects the result to a ReadonlyArray.

Explain briefly how Sinks aggregate Streams in Effect v3, then show a typed example that reduces a Stream<Order> to total revenue using Sink.foldLeft.

A Sink<A, In, L, E, R> consumes input elements In and produces a summary A, with leftover L and typed failure E. Running Stream.run(sink) pulls the source to completion and returns a single Effect<A, E, R>.
Write an Effect v3 program that aggregates a stream of temperatures into both min and max via Sink.zip. Constraints: strict types, no any, single stream pass.

Add a Sink.count consumer to this stream so we know how many items were processed alongside the collected array:

const out = pipe(src, Stream.run(Sink.collectAll<string>()));
Fix this Effect v3 snippet so it compiles: const total = Stream.make(1,2,3).runSum;
Build from scratch an fp-ts Option.alt pipeline that falls back between three primary/secondary/tertiary lookups, returning a final Option.
What's the fp-ts way to fall back from one Either to another on the left channel? Show a minimal example using E.alt.

Explain briefly how Option.alt works in fp-ts, then show a typed example that chooses between env-var, config-file, and default-constant sources for a port number.

O.alt is the applicative alternative for Option: if the first Option is None it takes the lazy fallback, otherwise it keeps the first.
Write an fp-ts function that looks up a user by id from cache then store, using Option.alt. Constraints: strict types, no any, pipe composition, single final O.getOrElse for a default.
Add a third fallback layer (hardcoded default) to this Option pipeline: const result = pipe(lookupA(k), O.alt(() => lookupB(k)));

Refactor this nested if chain to fp-ts Either.alt:

function load(k: string) { const a = fromA(k); if (a._tag === 'Right') return a; const b = fromB(k); if (b._tag === 'Right') return b; return fromC(k); }
Build from scratch a catamorphism over a simple recursive Expr (number | add | mul) using a pure fold.
What's the catamorphism pattern for a recursive List? Show a minimal typed example with a fold that computes the length.

Explain briefly how a catamorphism generalises fold over recursive data, then show a typed implementation that evaluates a boolean expression tree to a boolean.

A catamorphism is a fold parameterised by an algebra: for each constructor of the ADT you supply a pure case that maps children to a result; the recursion scheme handles the traversal. This lets you write totally different interpreters (evaluate, pretty-print, optimise) without touching the traversal code.
Write a catamorphism over a Tree<A> that computes the sum of all leaves. Constraints: strict types, no any, pure function, no mutation.
